summary: Matched the common fighter pass-through-platform action by replacing most of the relevant ft_08A4 assembly with a new C translation unit, `ftCo_Pass.c`, and wiring it into the build. The PR also added typed declarations and struct fields needed to express the behavior: pass motion vars at fp+2340/fp+2344, ftCommonData fields at +0x464..+0x47C, collision helpers `mpColl_8004CBC0`/`mpColl_8004CBE8`, and a typed `ft_80082F28` prototype. The match report in the PR body says the new file and all listed functions reached 100%. There were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Pass.c` with C implementations for `ft_80099F1C`, `ftCo_80099F9C`, `ft_8009A080`, `ft_8009A134`, `ft_8009A184`, `ftCo_8009A228`, `ftCo_Pass_Anim`, `ftCo_Pass_IASA`, `ftCo_Pass_Phys`, and `ftCo_Pass_Coll`.
Added `ftCo_Pass.h` declaring the matched pass-related functions with address comments and `ftCo_GObj*` signatures.

Removed the corresponding hand-written assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, leaving later functions in that asm file intact, and changed remaining asm call sites from local labels `.L_80099F9C`/`.L_8009A228` to symbols `ftCo_80099F9C`/`ftCo_8009A228`.
Added `ftCo_Pass.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` so the new C object is linked before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`.
Added the `pass` overlay to `union ftCommon_MotionVars` with fields at fp+2340 and fp+2344: `bool x0` and `float x4`.
Refined `struct ftCommonData` around offsets +0x464..+0x47C, replacing an opaque byte array with `float x464`, `float x468`, `float x46C`, `float x470`, and three `UNK_T` words.
Added prototypes for collision helpers `mpColl_8004CBC0(CollData*)` and `mpColl_8004CBE8(CollData*)`.
Changed `ft_80082F28` from an unknown prototype to `void ft_80082F28(ftCo_GObj* gobj)` in both the source asm wrapper and header.

Moved pass declarations out of `ft_0877.h` and into the dedicated `ftCo_Pass.h`; multiple fighter/common source files gained an include for `ftCommon/ftCo_Pass.h` where they reference these functions.
Changed `ftCo_800C60C8` in `ft_08A4.h` from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to `bool ftCo_800C60C8(ftCo_GObj* gobj)`.
Added a `MUST_MATCH` fallback definition of `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` as `asm { nop }` in `placeholder.h`.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated platform-pass input/collision test into `ft_80099F1C`, matching repeated assembly patterns that compare downward left-stick input against `p_ftCommonData->x464`, check `x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y` against `x468`, and call `mpColl_8004CBC0`.
Introduced a dedicated `ftCo_Pass` header instead of leaving pass-specific prototypes in the broad `ft_0877.h`, improving ownership of common action declarations.
Used the existing motion-var union pattern to name the fp+2340/fp+2344 overlay for pass state, allowing `ftCo_80099F9C` to express the one-shot pass flag and timer setup directly.
Preserved exact matching for stack-sensitive functions by adding `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack padding arrays with `/// @todo Unused stack` comments.
Translated the IASA function as a chain of early-return checks using `RETURN_IF`, closely reflecting the assembly sequence of calls that exits once an action input succeeds.
Updated residual assembly branch targets to real function symbols after moving code to C, avoiding fragile local-label references across the C/asm boundary.
Typed enough neighboring APIs (`ftCo_800C60C8`, `ft_80082F28`, `mpColl_8004CBC0`, `mpColl_8004CBE8`) to keep the matched C readable while avoiding broad speculative renaming.
decomp_lessons: When carving functions out of a large assembly file, update remaining assembly call sites to call the new C symbols explicitly; otherwise local-label references can hide cross-file dependencies.

Common-action matches often require small struct/header advances outside the new `.c` file: motion var overlays, common data offsets, collision helper prototypes, and caller includes all matter for a clean compile and link.

For unknown gameplay fields, offset-preserving names such as `x464`, `x468`, `x46C`, `x470`, `x0`, and `x4` are acceptable when semantics are not fully proven, especially if they unlock a verified match.
Use a dedicated action header (`ftCo_Pass.h`) once multiple fighters/common files need prototypes; this is preferable to leaving declarations in generic catch-all headers.
MUST_MATCH-only dummy locals can document stack artifacts without polluting normal builds, and should be marked as TODOs rather than treated as semantic variables.
Short action callback wrappers like `ftCo_Pass_Phys` and `ftCo_Pass_Coll` can match as direct delegates to existing physics/collision functions once their prototypes are correct.
naming_conventions: New common action files follow `ftCo_<Action>.c` and `ftCo_<Action>.h`, here `ftCo_Pass`.
Action callbacks use the common suffix convention `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`: `ftCo_Pass_Anim`, `ftCo_Pass_IASA`, `ftCo_Pass_Phys`, `ftCo_Pass_Coll`.
Still-unknown or not-yet-semantically-named functions retained address-style names such as `ft_80099F1C`, `ft_8009A080`, `ft_8009A134`, `ft_8009A184`, `ftCo_80099F9C`, and `ftCo_8009A228`.
The dedicated header guard used the address-style project convention `GALE01_099F1C`.
Struct fields without confirmed semantics retained offset-style names, e.g. `p_ftCommonData->x464`, `x468`, `x46C`, `x470`, and `fp->mv.co.pass.x0`/`x4`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed roughly 352 lines of pass-related assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` and replaced them with 135 lines of C in `ftCo_Pass.c`.
The PR body's match report lists `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Pass.c` and each included function at 100%.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` stack padding (`u8 _[8] = { 0 };`) in several functions to account for unused stack space seen in the assembly.

Preserved float and integer behavior from the assembly: downward input threshold uses `<= -p_ftCommonData->x464`, tilt timer compares against `x468`, pass vertical velocity uses `x46C`, and pass state timer uses `x470`.
Matched motion transitions through `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, including the `ftCo_MS_Pass` transition in `ftCo_8009A228` and a parameterized motion state in `ft_8009A184`.
After motion change, both transition helpers call `mpColl_8004CBE8(&fp->coll_data)` and set `fp->x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y = 0xFE`, mirroring the assembly side effects.
The IASA chain was expressed with `RETURN_IF(...)` calls for special-air, escape-air/item/attack-air/common checks, matching the assembly's repeated call-and-branch structure.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Can `fp->mv.co.pass.x0` and `fp->mv.co.pass.x4` be renamed once the pass state/timer semantics are confirmed?
Can `p_ftCommonData->x464`, `x468`, `x46C`, and `x470` be given semantic names related to platform pass input threshold, tilt timer, vertical velocity, and cooldown?
What are the precise semantics of `mpColl_8004CBC0` and `mpColl_8004CBE8` in platform pass collision handling?
Can `ft_80099F1C`, `ftCo_80099F9C`, `ft_8009A080`, `ft_8009A134`, `ft_8009A184`, and `ftCo_8009A228` be renamed after more adjacent ftCommon actions are understood?
Do other common action files need the `MUST_MATCH` unused-stack padding pattern used here?
Can remaining pass-related references in `ftCo_Squat_IASA` and `ftCo_SquatWait_IASA` be moved from assembly into C in a later match?
